name: Italy
excerpt: Italy became a nation-state in 1861 when the regional states of the peninsula, along with Sardinia and Sicily, were united under King Victor EMMANUEL II.

quick_facts.religions: Christian 80.8% (overwhelmingly Roman Catholic with very small groups of Jehovah's Witnesses and Protestants), Muslim 4.9%, unaffiliated 13.4%, other 0.9% (2020 est.)
quick_facts.ethnic_groups: Italian (includes small clusters of German-, French-, and Slovene-Italians in the north, Albanian-Italians, Croat-Italians, and Greek-Italians in the south)
quick_facts.climate: predominantly Mediterranean; alpine in far north; hot, dry in south
quick_facts.terrain: mostly rugged and mountainous; some plains, coastal lowlands
quick_facts.natural_resources: coal, antimony, mercury, zinc, potash, marble, barite, asbestos, pumice, fluorspar, feldspar, pyrite (sulfur), natural gas and crude oil reserves, fish, arable land
quick_facts.major_exports: packaged medicine, garments, cars, refined petroleum, vehicle parts/accessories (2023)

summaries.geography_summary: strategic location dominating central Mediterranean, as well as southern sea and air approaches to Western Europe
summaries.history_summary: Italy became a nation-state in 1861 when the regional states of the peninsula, along with Sardinia and Sicily, were united under King Victor EMMANUEL II. An era of parliamentary government came to a close in the early 1920s when Benito MUSSOLINI established a Fascist dictatorship. His alliance with Nazi Germany led to Italy's defeat in World War II. A democratic republic replaced the monarchy in 1946, and economic revival followed. Italy is a charter member of NATO, as well as the European Economic Community (EEC) and its successors, the EC and the EU. It has been at the forefront of European economic and political unification, joining the Economic and Monetary Union in 1999. Persistent problems include sluggish economic growth, high youth and female unemployment, organized crime, corruption, and economic disparities between southern Italy and the more prosperous north.
summaries.economy_summary: high-income, core EU economy; strong services, manufacturing, and tourism sectors; modest growth supported by net exports, low inflation, and public investments via EU funds; tight labor market with aging workforce and shortages in specialized skills; high public debt levels

suggested_reading: CIA World Factbook data (archived via factbook.json) | https://github.com/factbook/factbook.json

prayer_and_mission.prayer_intro: Italy's Catholic identity is deeply woven into national heritage as the historic seat of the Roman Catholic Church. Pray for the clear gospel of salvation by grace through faith alone to reach a nation where Catholic tradition and institutional authority run especially deep.
prayer_points: Pray for laborers to plant independent, Bible-believing local churches across a nation with very little evangelical presence.
Pray for Italians practicing inherited Catholic tradition to encounter genuine, personal faith in Christ.
Pray for Italian pastors to be trained in sound, Scripture-centered teaching.
Pray for gospel witness among Italy's growing Muslim immigrant population.
prayer_and_mission.mission_emphasis: As the historic seat of the Roman Catholic Church, Italy's Catholic identity runs especially deep, leaving very little evangelical presence. Ongoing needs include training national pastors in sound biblical doctrine and planting autonomous local churches practicing believer's baptism by immersion.
